_comment: Trigger eval for explaining skill. 10 should-trigger + 10 should-not-trigger = 20 cases. Skill gives the answer plus reasoning plus a pattern-recognition cue. Negative cases include how-to/procedural (different shape), simple lookups (no reasoning needed), and /working-through requests (user wants to derive the answer themselves).

evals.query: /explaining
why does the CAP theorem matter in real-world distributed systems?
explain to me why N+1 query problems are so common in ORM-based code, and how to spot them
I keep getting confused about when to use covariance vs correlation. can you help me actually understand the difference and when each one matters?
why is the dollar so dominant as a reserve currency? I want to actually understand the mechanism, not just the slogan
help me understand why startup valuations bounce between 'frothy' and 'depressed' on roughly seven-year cycles. what's actually driving that?
explain how futures contracts work — I want to understand them well enough to recognize when one is mispriced
I keep forgetting why we use cross-validation. give me the explanation that'll actually stick this time
why do most companies adopt microservices and then quietly walk it back two years later? what's the underlying dynamic?
explain why the chess world ended up with two competing rating systems (Elo vs FIDE adjustments) and what that tells us about scoring problems in general
how do I run a Postgres backup from the command line?
what's the capital of Estonia?
I want to figure this out myself — give me a framework but don't tell me the answer
summarize the latest paper on transformers in three bullets
fix this Python error: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pandas'
translate this French paragraph to English
what's 17% of 348?
draft an email to my manager about taking next Friday off
I just read about the Coase theorem. is it really that important or is it overrated?
give me a one-sentence definition of entropy
